commit.message:
docs(sdk): SDK 가이드 discuss→execute 플로우 수정 및 타입 정의 보완 (#145)

- 기본 사용법 예제에서 discuss() 결과를 execute()에 직접 전달하던 패턴 수정
  - discuss()는 { document, rounds, convergence }만 반환하므로
    execute()에 team과 tasks를 명시적으로 전달하는 올바른 패턴으로 변경
- execute() 섹션 "방법 1" 설명을 기획서+팀+태스크 조합 패턴으로 수정
- types/index.d.ts에 Discusser/Executor 누락 옵션 추가:
  - Discusser: parallelTiers, reviewModel
  - Executor: messageBus, worktreeIsolation, projectDir
  - MessageBus 인터페이스 신규 정의

closes #143, closes #144

Co-authored-by: Good Vibe Bot <sumsun-dev@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(internal): 파이프라인 프롬프트 셸 스크립트 JSON 인젝션 방지 (#147)

build_reviewer_prompt, build_ux_reviewer_prompt, build_ux_rereviewer_prompt
함수가 쉘 변수를 JSON에 직접 보간하던 패턴을 jq -nc --argjson으로 교체.
동일 파일 내 다른 함수들과 일관된 안전한 JSON 구성 방식으로 통일.

build_ux_rereviewer_prompt는 json_args를 구성하지만 사용하지 않던 버그도 함께 수정.

closes #146

Co-authored-by: Good Vibe Bot <sumsun-dev@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix az stdin consuming loop pipe, escape codes, add progress logging

- Use fd 3 for the VM while loop (done 3< <(...) / read <&3) so that
  az vm show cannot consume data from the loop's read source — this was
  the root cause of only 1 VM appearing despite 125 being found
- Switch terminal colors from raw ANSI sequences to tput so escape codes
  do not appear as literal text in terminals that do not support ANSI
- Add script PID to startup log (log "Script PID: $$") for monitoring
- Add per-VM progress counter in verbose mode [PID] VM N/total: name

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement glossary auto-extraction endpoint with Groq AI

- Add GlossaryService with Groq integration for term extraction

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto-dispatch build fixes + reconcile pr-sync interval

PR sync now uses a dedicated 10-minute cooldown instead of the 1-hour
dedup window, so syncs run on schedule. Playbook updated to detect
build failures and set buildStatus/buildFailReason fields.

Engine now auto-dispatches fix tasks when a PR has buildStatus: "failing",
routing back to the PR author agent. This completes the PR lifecycle:
create → review → fix feedback → fix build → merge detection.

commit.message:
Fix VM loop truncation, garbled table output, and unknown SQL fields

- Add || true to all jq/az calls inside the VM while loop so set -e
  does not exit the subshell early when a single VM call fails,
  causing only the first VM to appear in output
- Add local declaration for sql_license (missing, caused set -e exit)
- Strip \r from all az -o tsv captures (WSL/CRLF line endings caused
  garbled subscription names and escape codes in table rows)
- Support both top-level and .properties.* field paths for sqlImageSku,
  sqlImageOffer, sqlServerLicenseType (newer az CLI returns top-level)
- Add -c flag to jq -n entry builder for compact single-line output

commit.message:
Add GHA workflow to run adversarial fuzzer on Rust source changes

Triggers on push/PR to main when src/** or Cargo files change.
Builds release binary, installs R + mgcv, runs 100 fuzz trials.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fuzzer improvements and REML cost/gradient consistency fix

- Fix REML log|H| to use eigenvalue-thresholded decomposition matching
  the gradient, fixing cost/gradient mismatch that stalled BFGS on
  multi-penalty problems
- Fuzzer: real-time output (line-buffered stdout), per-engine timing
  (shown when >0.5s), full error dumps on failure, sort trials by
  estimated cost (smallest first), labeled metrics in output
- Fix mgcv gaulss predict handling (list vs matrix return type)
- Fix sinc divide-by-zero warning in fuzzer
- Bump fit_max_iter, refactor estimate/custom_family paths

commit.message:
feat: periodic PR status sync via ADO MCP polling

Every ~10 minutes, the engine dispatches a lightweight pr-sync task that
uses ADO MCP tools to check live status of all active PRs and update
local tracker files. This catches external approvals, merges, and review
votes that happen outside the squad.

Uses an existing agent (prefers Lambert) with a dedicated pr-sync
playbook. Only dispatches if there are active PRs and no sync is
already running.

- Addresses readability complaints about text being too transparent/dark

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: validation accuracy — use pre-dip peak, add post-pinch resistance, recalibrate presets

Three bugs caused validation deviations of 12-37% for I_peak:

1. Validation compared against global I_peak (including unphysical post-pinch
   oscillation) instead of the pre-dip physics peak (I_pre_dip). PF-1000
   went from 11.7% to 6.5% deviation with this fix alone.

2. Post-pinch snowplow returned dL_dt=0, F_magnetic=0, R_plasma=0. The RLC
   circuit oscillated with frozen inductance and no dissipation, producing
   unphysical current rise after pinch. Added Spitzer plasma resistance
   estimate (~2e-5 Ohm*m) to damp post-pinch current.

3. MJOLNIR and POSEIDON presets had uncalibrated Lee model parameters.
   MJOLNIR: f_m=0.10->0.45, f_c=0.70->0.55 (3.6% I_peak, 2.3% t_peak).
   POSEIDON: f_m=0.08->0.15 (2.7% I_peak).
   NX2: V0=11.5->11.0 kV (matching Lee & Saw 2008).

Results: 5/7 devices now PASS or FAIR (<=15% I_peak deviation).
PF-1000: 6.5%, POSEIDON: 2.7%, MJOLNIR: 3.6%, FAETON: 9.1%, UNU-ICTP: 10.6%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(desktop): bulletproof Electron launcher — never shows blank screen

- Add waitForUrl() polling to wait for Vite dev server before loading
- Show loading.html page immediately (splash → loading → app, never blank)
- Add did-fail-load retry logic (30 retries with 2s delay)
- Add continuousVitePoll() for auto-reconnect if Vite starts later
- Add dist/ fallback path for production mode
- Set appQuitting flag to cleanly stop all polling on shutdown
- Fix macOS close handler to allow quit when appQuitting is true

feat: self-service billing in setup wizard with tiered pricing

- Setup wizard step 9 now handles billing activation automatically
- Unlicensed users redirected to /setup instead of bare /activate page
- New POST /api/setup/activate endpoint: billing mode + promo key mode
- Billing mode calls provision_new_gym() → Square subscription auto-created
- Promo mode validates keygen key → free license, no billing

Tiered pricing (per-location, no unlimited loophole):
  Starter:  $299/mo (1 location, AI messaging only)
  Pro:      $499/mo first location
            $249/mo each additional (2-5 locations)
            $199/mo per location (6-20 locations)
            $149/mo per location (21-100 locations)
            Custom contract for 100+ locations

- 14-day free trial on all paid tiers (Square invoices after trial)
- Multi-location Pro creates named Square plan at calculated price
- Step 9 UI: side-by-side tier cards, live price calc, location count input
- Jeremy only needs to act to PREVENT billing, never to start it

commit.message:
fix: correct Worklytics metric key patterns from data dictionary

- google-chat uses hyphen prefix (google-chat:message:sent)
- Outlook email key is outlook-mail:emails:sent
- Calendar keys use gcal:/outlook-cal: prefixes with :hours:spent:meetings suffix
- Teams calls hours: msft:teams:v1:calls:sum:hours:spent:meetings
- Add Zoom meeting source option
- Focus/fragmented detection uses more specific worklytics: prefixed patterns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore wgpu/Metal renderer on macOS: fix transparency and black window

softbuffer on macOS uses kCGImageAlphaNoneSkipFirst which skips the alpha
byte entirely, making transparency structurally impossible. Switch back to
the wgpu/Metal renderer which hands alpha to Core Animation via Metal's
PostMultiplied composite mode.

- mod.rs: use renderer_macos (wgpu) instead of renderer_macos_softbuffer
- text_editing.rs: update Renderer import to match active macOS renderer
- Cargo.toml: restore wgpu = 28 (metal) as macOS-only dependency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Envato reference image upload and optimize bulk send speed

- Reference images from the app are now auto-uploaded to Envato's
  "Upload image references" modal (2 slots) via CORS fetch + DragEvent
- Added /tmp-ref CORS static route, writeRefImages() and generateRefUploadJS()
- Both single and bulk send endpoints accept referenceImages array
- Frontend collects first 2 uploaded images via collectAllImageData()
- Optimized bulk send: merged aspect ratio into single JS call,
  tightened polling (0.2→0.1s), reduced paste delay (0.8→0.5s),
  cut per-tab overhead from ~1.7s to ~0.9s

- Added Envato ImageGen/VideoGen full automation (image prompt → generate → video tab → settings → generate)
- Added sanitizeVideoPrompt() preserving Unicode accents for Spanish speech
- Added reference image upload via DragEvent for Envato page
- UI: Envato send buttons, Image→Video pipeline controls, bulk send
